title: chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. holguinensis
intro: Chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. holguinensis, also known as a rare and lesser-known species of the partridge pea plant, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its use as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. holguinensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and stems, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures.
